output.final_answer: Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and he passed away on February 24, 2001. Three key contributions he made to information theory include: 1) The development of the concept of the bit as a unit of information, 2) The formulation of the Shannon's theorem, which establishes the maximum data transmission rate of a communication channel, and 3) The introduction of the idea of digital circuits for implementing Boolean algebra.
